name: Chocolate Pepper Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: riacmt@ubvmsb.cc.buffalo.edu (Carol Miller-Tutzauer)
1. Cream the butter and sugar together until smooth.
2. Add the eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
3. Sift together the dry ingredients: flour, cocoa powder, black pepper, cayenne pepper, and cinnamon.
4. Gradually add the sifted mixture to the butter mixture, stirring until well combined. If the dough is too soft, add up to 1/4 cup more flour.
5. Shape the dough into a flattened round and wrap it in plastic. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
6. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
7. On a well-floured surface, roll out the dough until it is about 1/8 inch thick. It's best to divide and work with a quarter or third of the dough at a time, keeping the rest refrigerated.
8. Cut the rolled dough into desired shapes using cookie cutters. Place the cookies about 1 to 2 inches apart on the prepared baking sheets.
9. Bake for 8 to 10 minutes, or until crisp but not browned. Remove from the oven and let cool on wire racks.
10. Melt the chocolate according to package instructions. Drizzle over the cooled cookies in an irregular pattern. Allow the chocolate to harden completely before serving.
